examples.title: MPI Matrix-Vector Multiplication
examples.description: Parallel matrix-vector multiplication y = Ax using row-wise distribution. Each processor computes partial results for its rows, and results are gathered to the root process.
examples.code:
BEGIN
  // Assume A is n×n matrix, distributed by rows across P processors
  // Each process gets n/P rows of A and corresponding elements of y
  // Process 0 is root

  // Broadcast vector x to all processes
  IF rank == 0 THEN
    FOR i = 1 TO P-1 DO
      MPI_Send(x, n, MPI_DOUBLE, i, tag, comm)
    END FOR
  ELSE
    MPI_Recv(x, n, MPI_DOUBLE, 0, tag, comm, status)
  END IF

  // Local computation: y_local = A_local × x
  FOR i = 0 TO local_rows-1 DO
    y_local[i] = 0
    FOR j = 0 TO n-1 DO
      y_local[i] = y_local[i] + A_local[i][j] * x[j]
    END FOR
  END FOR

  // Gather all partial results to root
  IF rank == 0 THEN
    y[0:local_rows-1] = y_local
    FOR i = 1 TO P-1 DO
      MPI_Recv(y_buffer, n/P, MPI_DOUBLE, i, tag, comm, status)
      y[dest_offset:dest_offset+n/P-1] = y_buffer
    END FOR
  ELSE
    MPI_Send(y_local, local_rows, MPI_DOUBLE, 0, tag, comm)
  END IF
END
examples.explanation: The program distributes rows of matrix A across processors. Vector x must be broadcast to all workers. Each processor computes local dot products, and partial results are gathered to process 0. This demonstrates the typical SPMD pattern in distributed memory programming with collective communication for data distribution and result collection.

examples.title: Halo Exchange for 2D Stencil
examples.description: Ghost cell exchange between 4 neighboring processes in a 2D processor grid. Each process exchanges boundary rows and columns with neighbors before stencil computation.
examples.code:
BEGIN
  // Assume process has local domain with ghost cells
  // Grid dimensions: local_n × local_n (including ghost cells)
  // Neighbors: left, right, up, down (may be MPI_PROC_NULL for boundaries)

  // Exchange with left neighbor: send column 1, receive column n-2
  MPI_Sendrecv(local_domain[1:local_n-2][1], local_n-2, MPI_DOUBLE,
               left, tag_left,
               local_domain[1:local_n-2][local_n-1], local_n-2, MPI_DOUBLE,
               right, tag_right, comm, status)

  // Exchange with right neighbor: send column n-2, receive column 1
  MPI_Sendrecv(local_domain[1:local_n-2][local_n-2], local_n-2, MPI_DOUBLE,
               right, tag_right,
               local_domain[1:local_n-2][0], local_n-2, MPI_DOUBLE,
               left, tag_left, comm, status)

  // Exchange with top neighbor: send row 1, receive row n-2
  MPI_Sendrecv(local_domain[1][1:local_n-2], local_n-2, MPI_DOUBLE,
               up, tag_up,
               local_domain[local_n-1][1:local_n-2], local_n-2, MPI_DOUBLE,
               down, tag_down, comm, status)

  // Exchange with bottom neighbor: send row n-2, receive row 1
  MPI_Sendrecv(local_domain[local_n-2][1:local_n-2], local_n-2, MPI_DOUBLE,
               down, tag_down,
               local_domain[0][1:local_n-2], local_n-2, MPI_DOUBLE,
               up, tag_up, comm, status)

  // Now perform stencil computation on interior points
  FOR i = 2 TO local_n-3 DO
    FOR j = 2 TO local_n-3 DO
      new_domain[i][j] = (domain[i][j] + domain[i-1][j] + domain[i+1][j]
                        + domain[i][j-1] + domain[i][j+1]) * 0.2
    END FOR
  END FOR
END
examples.explanation: The code demonstrates halo exchange using MPI_Sendrecv which atomically performs send and receive to avoid deadlock. Ghost cells store boundary data from neighbors, enabling each process to compute stencil values using only local data. The interior computation loop excludes ghost cell boundaries.

examples.title: All-to-All Personalized Communication
examples.description: Complete exchange where every process sends a unique message to every other process. Demonstrates the most general communication pattern in distributed memory systems.
examples.explanation: All-to-all communication is used in matrix transpose, FFT, and collective algorithms. Each process sends a unique message to every other process, and receives unique messages from all. The MPI_Alltoall call completes in O(P × msg_size) time, representing O(P²) total message volume across the system.
examples.timeComplexity: O(P × m) where m is message size per pair
